name: Contradiction Resolution
description: Tests that NeuroMem correctly invalidates old preferences when the user changes their mind. Standard RAG would retrieve both conflicting facts.

conversation.content: Hi! My name is Alex. I absolutely love pizza — it's my favorite food in the world.
Nice to meet you, Alex! A fellow pizza lover — great taste! Do you have a favorite style or topping?
I work as a data scientist at a company called NovaTech.
Data science at NovaTech — sounds like interesting work! What kind of projects are you involved in?
Actually, I've completely changed my mind about pizza. I hate it now. My favorite food is sushi.
Taste buds can definitely change! Sushi is a fantastic choice. Do you have a favorite type — nigiri, sashimi, or rolls?

test_queries.description: Should return sushi, NOT pizza. Old preference must be invalidated.
Non-contradicted fact should be recalled accurately.

rag_expected_failure: Standard RAG retrieves both 'loves pizza' and 'favorite is sushi', confusing the LLM. NeuroMem's contradiction detector invalidates the old preference.
neuromem_advantage: Knowledge Graph temporal invalidation + contradiction detection
